title: Admin Checks Operational Reports
content_html:
Admin Checks Operational Reports

Status: proposed feature

Summary

This feature captures Admin Checks reports that answer operational, runtime, payload, performance, or generated-artifact questions.

It is intentionally separate from Admin Checks Responsibility Reports, which is scoped to responsibility and ownership evidence.

Operational reports may still use the Admin Checks runner, report registry, artifacts, and UI, but they should not broaden the responsibility-focused feature.

Scope

This feature can contain deliveries that answer questions such as:

Question                                      Evidence report
--------------------------------------------  ------------------
Which generated payloads are large or broad?  generated-payloads
Which targets have runtime evidence gaps?     runtime-checks
Which route profiles are stale or failing?    runtime-checks
Which payloads or routes need perf review?    future delivery

These reports should remain narrow evidence reports. They should not become a catch-all test dashboard, and they should not declare responsibility risk unless a later dependency report explicitly joins them with ownership evidence.

Boundary

Operational report deliveries belong here when their primary question is about:

  • generated payload size, shape, or validity
  • runtime profile freshness or status
  • performance measurements
  • route smoke results
  • build or generated-artifact health

Responsibility report deliveries belong under Admin Checks Responsibility Reports when their primary question is about:

  • file size as maintainability evidence
  • configured ownership and target boundaries
  • imports across target boundaries
  • public or top-level code surface
  • churn around selected source files
  • discovered test links around selected source files
  • configured responsibility or ownership tokens

Non-Goals

  • no catch-all dashboard scope
  • no browser-defined commands, search roots, or profiles
  • no replacement for focused report deliveries
  • no broad risk score from operational metrics alone
